name: Alberche Greenway and Picadas Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalses de San Juan y Picadas – circular desde Pelayos de la Presa – gravel
description: A difficult 19.7-mile gravel cycling route from Pelayos de la Presa, exploring the scenic San Juan and Picadas Reservoirs.
region_intro: Touring cycling around San Martín De Valdeiglesias offers diverse landscapes within Madrid's Sierra Oeste, characterized by the Alberche River and its two major reservoirs, San Juan and Picadas. The region features Mediterranean forests with holm oaks, cork oaks, and extensive pine trees, providing varied scenery for cyclists. Gradients are generally less extreme compared to other mountainous areas, making routes accessible for different experience levels. Many routes utilize former railway lines, such as the Vía Verde del Alberche, offering scenic paths through canyons and along the water's edge. Best touring cycling routes around San Martín De Valdeiglesias The most popular touring cycling route is Alberche Greenway and Picadas Reservoir Loop, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail that takes about 3 hours to complete. This route follows the scenic Alberche Greenway and circles the Picadas Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Coracera Castle – Río Alberche loop from San Juan, a difficult 29.9 miles (48.2 km) path. This route explores the area around Coracera Castle and follows the Río Alberche. Local touring cyclists also love the Alberche Greenway – Picadas Reservoir loop from San Martín de Valdeiglesias, a 38.5 miles (61.9 km) trail leading through the Alberche Greenway and along the Picadas Reservoir, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around San Martín De Valdeiglesias is defined by the Alberche River, the San Juan and Picadas reservoirs, and Mediterranean pine forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in San Martín De Valdeiglesias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore San Martín De Valdeiglesias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in San Martín de Valdeiglesias?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes in San Martín de Valdeiglesias, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The routes in San Martín de Valdeiglesias cater to various experience levels. You'll find 5 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 28 more challenging routes, ensuring there's something for everyone from beginners to advanced cyclists.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the region's generally less extreme gradients compared to other mountainous areas make it accessible for families and beginners. There are 5 easy routes available, and many paths wind through pine forests and along the water's edge, offering pleasant and manageable rides.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in San Martín de Valdeiglesias?
A: You can expect diverse landscapes defined by the Alberche River and its two major reservoirs, San Juan and Picadas. Routes often pass through Mediterranean forests with holm oaks, cork oaks, and extensive pine trees, offering varied and scenic views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959907903/alberche-greenway-and-picadas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberche Greenway and Picadas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that follows the scenic Alberche Greenway and circles the Picadas Reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31882336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberche Greenway – Picadas Reservoir loop from San Martín de Valdeiglesias&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I cycle along the reservoirs or rivers in San Martín de Valdeiglesias?
A: Absolutely. The San Juan and Picadas reservoirs, along with the Alberche River, are central to many cycling routes. You'll find paths winding along the water's edge, offering beautiful views and opportunities for water sports at San Juan Reservoir's designated bathing areas.

Q: Are there any routes that utilize former railway lines?
A: Yes, several routes in the region utilize former railway lines, such as the Vía Verde del Alberche. These greenways offer scenic and generally favorable paths for cyclists, often passing through canyons and recreational areas.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while touring cycling in San Martín de Valdeiglesias?
A: You can explore several historical sites. The prominent 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31891886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coracera Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark. The town also boasts an old quarter with narrow streets, the Church of San Martín de Obispo, and several hermitages. The region is also known for its wineries, being part of the D.O. Vinos de Madrid.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can find natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Abuelo Chestnut Tree (Castañar de El Tiemblo)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5014999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Pine Group&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider routes near summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5149766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Almenara&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5766701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Cebreros and Cerro Guisando&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in San Martín de Valdeiglesias?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the Mediterranean climate suggests spring and autumn would offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be hot, and winters mild, but always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in San Martín de Valdeiglesias?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the presence of the Alberche River and reservoirs, and the variety of routes that cater to different abilities.

Q: Are there any advanced touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists, there are 28 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38186117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trasierra loop from San Martín de Valdeiglesias&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging ride with significant elevation changes.
tips: The Picadas reservoir is located between the municipalities of Pelayos de la Presa and Aldea del Fresno, about 60 km from Madrid, in the Sierra de Gredos. This reservoir collects the waters of the Alberche River and has a recreational area that the whole family will enjoy.

A tunnel carved into the rock that is part of a railway that never worked.

Today it is a hiking and biking route that takes you through bridges and tunnels like this one, following the course of the Alberche River and the Picadas Reservoir, right next to the San Juan Reservoir.

https://bigjoe.es

The San Juan reservoir is located in the municipalities of San Martín de Valdeiglesias, El Tiemblo, Cebreros and Pelayos de la Presa, in the southwestern end of the Community of Madrid and southeast of Ávila.
It collects the waters of the Alberche and Cofio rivers.
It is accessed through the M-501, the "road of the marshes".
https://bigjoe.es

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this gravel cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging route is approximately 31.6 km (19.6 miles) long and typically takes almost three hours to complete for touring cyclists with a very good fitness level.

faqs.question: Is this gravel cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires a 'very good fitness level'. It features significant gravel sections and varied terrain, making it more suitable for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: Is the San Juan Reservoirs and Picadas route a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning you will end up back at your starting point in Pelayos de la Presa.

faqs.question: Where does the San Juan Reservoirs and Picadas route start?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Pelayos de la Presa, offering convenient access to the circular gravel trail.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features diverse and varied terrain, including significant gravel sections. A notable part follows the old Alberche train route, offering a favorable path through pine forests and along the shore of the Picadas reservoir. You will also encounter a small slope to cross at the dam before descending to the Alberche River bridge.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse landscapes surrounding the Embalse de San Juan and the Alberche river. The route offers a unique aquatic backdrop from the reservoir, picturesque pine forests, and the beautiful shoreline of the Picadas Reservoir. You'll also pass by the San Juan Reservoir Marina and a Tunnel on the Alberche Greenway.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations aren't provided, considering the varied terrain and gravel sections, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling in this region, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential winter conditions.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the Vía Verde del Alberche, Camino de Villa del Prado a Pelayos, and Cañada de Talavera o del Puente de San Juan.

faqs.question: Is the San Juan Reservoirs and Picadas route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for cycling routes can vary. It's best to check local regulations for the Embalse de San Juan area regarding dogs on trails, especially around reservoirs and protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access this specific cycling route.

faqs.question: Is there parking available near the trailhead in Pelayos de la Presa?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pelayos de la Presa serves as the starting point, and you can typically find parking options within the town to access the route.
